summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions across northern and central regions while downing over 100 hostile drones, including Shahed and Iskander‑M missile attacks from Rostov. Significant strikes hit Russian oil infrastructure, notably the Sizran refinery, and Ukrainian special forces destroyed a Russian air‑defence complex in occupied Kherson. Front‑line evacuations continue as combat persists in Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv oblasts.
key_points: Ukrainian air defence shot down 109 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Iskander‑M missile attacks from Rostov, across north, south and east (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Russian Iskander‑M ballistic missiles were launched from Taganrog/Rostov region, threatening Ukrainian territories (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66882).
Ukrainian special operations and SBS destroyed the Russian Pantsir‑S1 air‑defence system in occupied Kherson region (Source: @UkraineNow/81365).
Ukrainian forces struck the Sizran oil refinery (~800 km from the front), causing a large fire that impacts fuel supplies for Russian military aviation (Source: @UkraineNow/81362).
Multiple Shahed drones were detected over Kyiv suburbs and towns: Vyshneve to Bilohradi (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66890), Brovary (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66886), and Zhulyany (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66888).

Ukrainian UAVs were reported moving from Poltava toward Lino‑vitsia, and from Dnipropetrovsk toward Girnytske and Chortomlyk, indicating offensive strike groups (Source: @eRadarrua/92864, @eRadarrua/92861).
Evacuation orders issued for Chaplyne and Shevchenkove villages, 26‑30 km from the front line in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30325).
Five civilians injured in a Russian attack on Dnipro, with two hospitalized, and damage to two high‑rise buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81369).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defences downed over 100 hostile UAVs, including Shahed drones and an Iskander‑M missile strike from Rostov, highlighting intense aerial pressure. Ukrainian special forces successfully destroyed a Russian Pantsir‑S1 system in occupied Kherson and struck the strategic Sizran oil refinery, degrading Russian fuel logistics. Front‑line evacuations and civilian casualties persist as combat operations continue across Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv and central Ukraine.

points: Ukrainian air defence units reported shooting down 109 hostile UAVs of various types (Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas) across the north, south and east, with confirmed hits on five locations on 21‑22 May (Source: @kpszsu/62362).

Special operations forces, together with the Security Service, hit the Sizran oil refinery (capacity up to 9 Mt/yr) located over 800 km from the front, igniting a large fire that threatens fuel supplies for Russian aviation (Source: @UkraineNow/81362).
Ukrainian forces destroyed a Russian Pantsir‑S1 air‑defence complex in occupied Kherson oblast, reportedly causing around 100 Russian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81365).

Multiple Shahed drones were tracked over Kyiv suburbs: one from Vyshneve toward Bilohradi, another over Brovary, and a third over Zhulyany, prompting local air‑defence alerts (Sources: @mon1tor_ua/66890, @mon1tor_ua/66886, @mon1tor_ua/66888).

UAV strike groups were observed moving from Poltava toward Lino‑vitsia and from Dnipropetrovsk toward Girnytske, Chortomlyk, and other targets, indicating coordinated offensive operations (Sources: @eRadarrua/92864, @eRadarrua/92861).
Five civilians were injured in a Russian attack on Dnipro, with two hospitalized and damage to two high‑rise residential buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81369).
Evacuation orders were issued for Chaplyne and Shevchenkove villages, 26‑30 km from the front line in Dnipropetrovsk region, reflecting shifting front‑line dynamics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30325).
Ukrainian intelligence reported new UAV groups approaching Sumy region near V.Pisarivka, expanding pressure on the northeastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/92824).
Russian forces launched an Iskander‑M ballistic missile from the Taganrog/Rostov area, targeting Ukrainian positions and prompting air‑defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66882).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the eastern and northeastern directions, with threats of air‑strike missions over front‑line oblasts (Sources: @kpszsu/62384, @kpszsu/62375).
A Russian air‑defence complex (Pantsir‑S1) in occupied Kherson was destroyed by Ukrainian special forces, reducing Russian low‑altitude air‑defence coverage (Source: @UkraineNow/81365).
Russian missile remnants recovered in Chernihiv region showed elevated radiation, suggesting use of a nuclear‑capable or radiological payload on a Geran‑2 drone (Source: @UkraineNow/81359).
Russian UAV groups were reported moving from Kursk and Donetsk regions toward Sumy and Dnipropetrovsk, indicating continued offensive drone operations (Source: @kpszsu/62342).
Russian forces suffered significant personnel losses (≈100) in the Kherson air‑defence engagement, reflecting mounting Ukrainian pressure (Source: @UkraineNow/81365).

Russian oil refineries in central Russia, including Kirishi, Ryazan, Yaroslavl and Nizhny Novgorod, reported shutdowns after Ukrainian strikes, cutting roughly 25‑30% of Russian fuel output (Source: @UkraineNow/81355).
Russian tactical aviation was also active on the southeastern front, posing threats to Ukrainian rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/62336).
UAV groups from Kursk and Donetsk were directed toward Sumy and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, increasing aerial pressure on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/62342).

Ukrainian strike drones were observed moving from Poltava toward Lino‑vitsia and from Dnipropetrovsk toward Girnytske and Chortomlyk, indicating offensive actions in the east (Sources: @eRadarrua/92864, @eRadarrua/92861).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the eastern direction, with threats of air‑strike missions over front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/62384).
Shahed drones were detected over Kyiv suburbs, including Vyshneve to Bilohradi, suggesting spill‑over of eastern UAV routes toward central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66890).
Ukrainian forces reported new UAV groups approaching Sumy near V.Pisarivka, expanding the eastern aerial contest (Source: @eRadarrua/92824).
Russian Iskander‑M missile launches from Rostov threatened eastern Ukrainian territories, adding a ballistic component to the front (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66882).
Ukrainian evacuation of villages near the front line in Dnipropetrovsk reflects shifting control in the eastern sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30325).
Five civilian casualties in Dnipro from a Russian attack underscore the ongoing threat to eastern urban centers (Source: @UkraineNow/81369).
Ukrainian air defence downed 109 hostile UAVs across the north, including Shahed, Gerbera and Italmas types, on 21‑22 May (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Shahed drones were tracked over Brovary and Zhulyany, prompting alerts in the northern Kyiv suburbs (Sources: @mon1tor_ua/66886, @mon1tor_ua/66888).
UAV activity from Kursk heading toward Sumy increased northern pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62342).
Ukrainian radar reported a Shahed drone launching from Kyiv heading toward Vyshneve and Bilohradi (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66890).
Russian tactical aviation was active on the northeastern direction, threatening front‑line oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/62375).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed moving from Poltava toward Lino‑vitsia, potentially crossing northern operational zones (Source: @eRadarrua/92864).
Radiation‑elevated debris from a Russian Geran‑2 drone was found in Chernihiv, indicating use of potentially hazardous payloads in the north (Source: @UkraineNow/81359).
Ukrainian forces continue to monitor and engage UAV incursions near Kyiv, maintaining a high‑alert air‑defence posture (Source: @eRadarrua/92858).
Ukrainian special forces destroyed a Russian Pantsir‑S1 air‑defence system in occupied Kherson, reducing southern air‑defence coverage (Source: @UkraineNow/81365).
UAV groups were reported moving from Dnipropetrovsk toward Girnytske, Chortomlyk and Kam'yanka‑Dniprovska in the south, indicating offensive strike activity (Source: @eRadarrua/92861).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the southeastern direction, posing threats to Ukrainian southern rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/62336).
Ukrainian forces liberated villages on the Kharkiv front, pushing Russian units toward the state border, a development affecting southern logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81364).
Russian Iskander‑M missile launches from Taganrog/Rostov threatened southern Ukrainian regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66882).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed heading from Dnipropetrovsk toward Kam'yanka‑Dniprovska, a key southern corridor (Source: @eRadarrua/92861).
Five civilian injuries in Dnipro, a southern industrial hub, underscore ongoing Russian strike risk (Source: @UkraineNow/81369).
Ukrainian air‑defence engagements in the south contributed to the overall tally of 109 downed UAVs (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Ukrainian forces shot down 109 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and drone‑imitators, across multiple fronts on 21‑22 May (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Russian tactical aviation was active on eastern, northeastern, southeastern and southern directions, indicating a broad air‑strike campaign (Sources: @kpszsu/62384, @kpszsu/62375, @kpszsu/62336).
Iskander‑M ballistic missiles were launched from Taganrog/Rostov, adding a high‑speed, long‑range threat to Ukrainian air‑defence (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66882).

Ukrainian air‑defence intercepted Shahed drones over Kyiv suburbs (Brovary, Zhulyany, Vyshneve) and central Ukraine, maintaining defensive coverage (Sources: @mon1tor_ua/66886, @mon1tor_ua/66888, @mon1tor_ua/66890).
Radiation‑elevated debris from a Russian Geran‑2 drone was recovered in Chernihiv, suggesting use of unconventional payloads (Source: @UkraineNow/81359).
Ukrainian UAV strike groups operated from Poltava and Dnipropetrovsk toward enemy positions, showing offensive air‑capability (Sources: @eRadarrua/92864, @eRadarrua/92861).
Russian UAV groups from Kursk and Donetsk moved toward Sumy and Dnipropetrovsk, sustaining pressure on Ukrainian airspace (Source: @kpszsu/62342).
Ukrainian special forces eliminated a Russian Pantsir‑S1 system in occupied Kherson, degrading Russian low‑altitude air‑defence (Source: @UkraineNow/81365).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defences downed 109 hostile UAVs, including Shahed and Iskander‑M missile attacks from Rostov, across multiple fronts, demonstrating robust anti‑air capability (Source: @kpszsu/62362).
Ukrainian special forces destroyed a Russian Pantsir‑S1 air‑defence complex in occupied Kherson, reducing Russian low‑altitude coverage (Source: @UkraineNow/81365).

A strike by Ukrainian special operations and SBS ignited a large fire at the Sizran oil refinery (~800 km from the front), disrupting fuel supplies for Russian military aviation (Source: @UkraineNow/81362).
Russian Iskander‑M ballistic missiles were launched from Taganrog/Rostov, targeting Ukrainian positions and prompting heightened air‑defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66882).

Multiple Shahed drones were detected over Kyiv suburbs (Brovary, Zhulyany, Vyshneve), indicating continued Russian UAV pressure on central Ukraine (Sources: @mon1tor_ua/66886, @mon1tor_ua/66888, @mon1tor_ua/66890).

Ukrainian UAV strike groups moved from Poltava toward Lino‑vitsia and from Dnipropetrovsk toward Girnytske and Chortomlyk, showing offensive UAV operations (Sources: @eRadarrua/92864, @eRadarrua/92861).
Evacuation orders for Chaplyne and Shevchenkove villages, 26‑30 km from the front line in Dnipropetrovsk, reflect shifting front‑line dynamics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30325).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces continue to employ a mixed aerial threat package, combining high‑speed Iskander‑M ballistic missiles, Shahed suicide drones and tactical aviation across all fronts, challenging Ukrainian air‑defence. Ukrainian counter‑measures have proven effective, downing over 100 UAVs and degrading key Russian air‑defence assets, notably the Pantsir‑S1 in Kherson. The targeting of Russian oil infrastructure, especially the Sizran refinery, indicates a strategic shift to undermine Russian logistical sustainment, while front‑line evacuations suggest ongoing territorial fluidity in the Dnipropetrovsk sector.
sections.regional_response: Western allies have reaffirmed support, with statements encouraging Ukrainian strikes on Russian targets, while Russia maintains its stance against an arms race, focusing on missile deployments from Rostov. Ukrainian authorities are coordinating civilian evacuations near the front and reinforcing air‑defence networks in the north and central regions. Russian economic pressure persists, evidenced by large‑scale refinery shutdowns, yet Ukraine's operational tempo remains high across multiple fronts.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV launch points in Kursk, Donetsk and Rostov is critical for early warning. The emergence of radiological signatures on drone debris in Chernihiv warrants investigation into possible unconventional payloads. Ukrainian offensive UAV movements toward Lino‑vitsia and Girnytske suggest preparation for deeper strikes, requiring updated threat matrices. Russian Iskander‑M launch sites near Taganrog should be prioritized for counter‑strike targeting to reduce ballistic missile threats.
